summary:
Severe pre-eclampsia necessitates prompt control of high BP using medications such as labetalol or hydralazine; also administer magnesium sulfate to prevent eclamptic seizures while planning an early cesarean section post-stabilizing mother’s health status at this late stage of pregnancy. To summarize:

The patient requires rapid stabilization through administration of intravenous antihypertensives like labetalol or hydralazine along with magnesium sulfate for neuroprotection against seizures. Early induction or Cesarean delivery should be planned given her advanced gestational age and severely elevated blood pressures indicative of impending complications from severe pre-eclampsia.
